testSummary: Cold full Vitest suite GREEN: 43/43 task packages successful, 1222 individual tests passed, 0 failed (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp pnpm test). Integration tiers ran against REAL Postgres (cdp); SES/AWS mocked (CountingSes); webhook HTTP client injected (fakeWebhookClient). pnpm typecheck clean (43/43, FULL TURBO). The consolidated journey entrypoint services/local-api/test/automation-journey-live-enroll.integration.test.ts passes (2 tests) and is non-vacuous: enrolls via ALL THREE trigger kinds (LIVE POST /profiles/:id/events through createApp→sendProfileEvent→enrollFromEvent, manual enrollProfileManually, segment enrollSegmentSnapshot), advances trigger→wait(1h)→hour_of_day_window(9..17, America/New_York DST-correct)→if(total_events>=1)→set_attribute(literal welcomed=y)→set_attribute(event-sourced last_purchase_amount={{event.amount}}=42.5 from persisted enrollment.state.event)→send→webhook→exit with an INJECTED clock (deps.now fixed per tick), asserts the send flows outbox→REAL Dispatcher→messages_log(automation_id) with From resolved from sender_id ('Acme Team' <team@mail.acme.com>) + subject/To/body merge rendered, the injected webhook fires EXACTLY once to the allowlisted https://hooks.example.com/hook with the secret decrypted at call-time only (activity_log detail never contains the plaintext token), idempotent re-tick (skipped; no 2nd outbox/send/webhook), and a parallel WS_B automation/enrollment stays untouched (still at trigger, zero messages_log/outbox).
e2eSummary: Solo Playwright (cd web && pnpm exec playwright test, single invocation, ports 8788/5174 free, cdp_e2e re-seeded). First full run surfaced ONE genuine failure (failed on retry too) in the UNRELATED spec e2e/profile-detail.browser.e2e.spec.ts 'filter the profiles list by manual and dynamic segments' — it asserted toHaveCount(4) profile-rows but the seed now legitimately adds 2 automation-enrollment fixture profiles (enr-active@acme.com, enr-completed@acme.com) to WS_A so the automations list shows non-zero counts → 6 rows. This is a seed-vs-stale-assertion regression introduced by the automation-builder branch, not a automation-feature bug. Fixed by updating the two stale toHaveCount(4)→(6) assertions + their comment (the filtered Manual VIPs=1 / VIP dynamic=2 / unsub=1 assertions are unaffected since the new profiles carry no tier/unsubscribed attribute and no segment membership). Re-ran the spec in isolation: 7/7 green. Final full e2e run: 100 passed, 1 flaky (e2e/company-ses-config.browser.e2e.spec.ts — 'element is not enabled' under full-suite API load, recovered on retry; exactly the unrelated spec named as an acceptable retry-recovered flake). NO genuine automation/broadcast/segment failures (no failure artifacts for those specs). The journey browser smoke (web/e2e/automation-journey-smoke.browser.e2e.spec.ts) builds the full-node journey via the (+) palette, configures each node, attaches a sendable seeded template + allowlisted webhook, saves and PUBLISHES to active, then confirms active + enrollment-counts on the list — passed.
entrypoint: Consolidated journey acceptance: services/local-api/test/automation-journey-live-enroll.integration.test.ts (run: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api exec vitest run test/automation-journey-live-enroll.integration.test.ts). Browser smoke: web/e2e/automation-journey-smoke.browser.e2e.spec.ts (run: cd web && pnpm exec playwright test).
commandsRun: cd web && pnpm exec playwright test
cd web && pnpm exec playwright test e2e/profile-detail.browser.e2e.spec.ts
notes: Cross-cutting guarantees re-proven at the system level (cited tests): TENANT ISOLATION across all automation paths — automation-journey-live-enroll (WS_B automation/enrollment untouched by WS_A ticks; zero WS_B messages_log/outbox), services/local-api/test/cross-workspace-action.integration.test.ts + automation-enroll-endpoint (cross-workspace automation/profile/segment/sender ids refused, inv.2), tests/acceptance/penetration-isolation.test.ts. RUNNER IDEMPOTENCY/CONCURRENCY with the pause gate — services/automation-runner/test/idempotent-concurrent-sweep.integration.test.ts, optimistic-advance-claim.test.ts, webhook-idempotency.integration.test.ts, paused-automation-not-advanced.integration.test.ts, plus the journey re-tick (skipped, no double send/webhook, single outbox row). WEBHOOK SSRF/ALLOWLIST + injected client — services/automation-runner/test/webhook-node-tick.integration.test.ts (+ the journey: allowlisted host only, secret decrypted at call-time, plaintext never persisted, failure isolated). WORKSPACE-TZ TIME MATH (DST-correct) — services/automation-runner/test/hour-window.integration.test.ts + the journey's NY-zone wait→window park/resume across the t0/t2/t3 clocks. ALL AUTOMATION SENDS THROUGH THE DISPATCHER GATING — services/automation-runner/test/action-send-through-dispatcher + automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts + services/local-api/test/automation-activate-gate / automation-publish-gate (From sender + To + Subject mandatory, no no-reply fallback, verified-domain gate) + the journey's real dispatchOutbox→messages_log(automation_id). The single code change is test-only (the stale e2e assertion fix above); no app/runner/dispatcher/migration changes were needed — the phase 1-7 implementation already satisfies the full end-to-end story. Root version already at the agreed 0.36.0; CLAUDE.md current (25 automation references covering the builder, DSL nodes, tz, triggers). DB: cdp-pg up on :5433; migrations already applied to cdp and cdp_e2e (e2e re-seeds each run and all schema-dependent specs pass). Did NOT push (per instruction).
